topic: Gemini CLI vs Codex CLI 2026: Google's Free Tier vs OpenAI's Rust-Built Agent

key_points: Gemini CLI falls short on terminal-native task performance vs Codex
Codex CLI Rust rewrite gives noticeably faster startup and token processing
Gemini CLI wins on context window (1M tokens) and free tier generosity
Codex leads on CI/CD integration out of the box
Codex CLI is laser-focused on code workflows; Gemini acts more like a multi-domain AI agent
Gemini CLI integrates with IDEs via VS Code extension and Agent Client Protocol (ACP)
Codex CLI supports MCP, subagents, image input, and kernel-level sandboxing
Codex CLI uses 4x fewer tokens than Claude Code for equivalent tasks
Gemini CLI: 1,000 requests/day free, 60 req/min, no credit card required
Codex CLI: free CLI tool but API usage billed per token (approx $3-4 per medium task with o3)
Gemini CLI added Plan Mode (March 2026): read-only strategy phase before writing files
Codex CLI scored 77.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, leading by 12-point margin
Codex CLI ships codex exec for CI pipelines and a GitHub Action for automated PR review
Gemini CLI uses Google Search grounding for staying current with best practices
Both tools are open source and available for free use
Gemini CLI best for large codebases on a budget; Codex best for autonomous batch ops
Codex CLI rebuilt in Rust (~95% of codebase), dramatically reducing startup time and memory
Gemini CLI context window (1M tokens standard) vs Codex default 272K (configurable to 1M)
Team pricing: Gemini CLI $19/user/month vs Codex CLI ~$25/user/month
Codex CLI has 640+ tagged releases, 5,075+ commits, 400+ contributors, 9,000 forks

stats.fact: Gemini CLI offers 1,000 requests/day and 60 requests/minute free with a personal Google account — no credit card required
Codex CLI scored 77.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, leading competitors by a 12-point margin on terminal-native task performance
Codex CLI is ~95% Rust codebase after the June 2025 rewrite from TypeScript, reducing startup time and memory usage significantly
Gemini CLI has 3.2 million monthly npm downloads vs Codex CLI's 14.0 million — placing Gemini CLI third among major AI coding CLIs
Gemini CLI's standard context window is 1M tokens (Gemini 2.5 Pro); Codex CLI defaults to 272K but is configurable up to 1M tokens
Codex CLI has shipped 640+ tagged releases (roughly one per day since launch) and accumulated 5,075+ commits from 400+ contributors
For a 10-person team, Gemini CLI costs ~$190/month ($19/user) vs Codex CLI ~$250/month ($25/user)
Gemini 2.5 Pro API pricing: $1.00 per million input tokens, $10.00 per million output tokens (paid tier beyond free quota)

angles: Free-tier shootout: Gemini's 1,000 req/day with no billing vs Codex's per-token API costs — which is truly free for solo devs?
